---
title: "tbox-finder — Phase 2 (Stage-1 training) dev-log"
author: "bioedca"
format:
  typst:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

<!-- Rendered with the Typst engine (quarto >=1.9, `format: typst`): the
     laptop/CI toolchain ships no LaTeX, and Typst produces the PDF natively.
     Renders via `conda run -n tbox-finder-viz quarto render analyses/phase2_log.qmd`.
     NOTE (P1 exit-gate lesson): a bare `at`-prefixed token (a length like
     "at-300", a revision like "at-2c2c5c14") is parsed by pandoc as a citation
     key against a doc with no bibliography and kills the render — escape or
     rephrase any such token rather than writing it bare. -->

# Phase 2 — Stage-1 training

Per-step development log (CLAUDE.md §2.4). One stanza is appended after every
step's commit; this file renders to `docs/dev-log/phase2_<YYYY-MM-DD>.pdf` at
the phase-exit gate.

Phase-2 goal (imp.md): train/calibrate the genomic segmenter (production scanner
plus the class-II-CM-naive ablation), run the §9.1 hard-negative-mining loop, and
grade **GATE-4** — the minimum per-element per-nucleotide F1 over the three core
elements {Stem I, Specifier, Antiterminator}, floor >= 0.80 (ADR-0004 D6). GATE-4
is the project's **first generalization grade**; every Phase-1 number was a
learnability/expressivity smoke on held-out loci and must not be quoted as
generalization (CLAUDE.md §10.3).

## Step P2-00 — 2026-07-16: source real flanking genomic context for the training corpus

**Inputs.** `data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet` (23,535 records; `Name` = `accession:start-end`, 11,633 accessions) and the git-LFS `data/processed/splits/split_assignments.parquet` (the identity anchor — `ingest.compute_record_hashes(master)` must reproduce its `corpus_record_sha256` set, fail-loud). NCBI `nuccore` via E-utilities.

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/data/flank_context.py::build_flank_context` (rule `workflow/rules/data.smk::p2_flank_context`) exists because PRD §6 pins Stage-1 training positives as offset-augmented placements in a **1024-nt window**, but the corpus stores each locus as `FASTA_sequence` = 104–550 nt of dense DNA (median 281) with negligible native flank (median 0 / 50 nt; only 5/23,535 reach 1024 nt) and `data/raw/` is empty. Synthesising the remainder was rejected on the science (a §9.1 GC-matched 0th-order splice is a trivially separable boundary cue that would inflate GATE-4 and GATE-1 — PRD §5). So the flank is **sourced** (PRD §10.2). The stored coordinates are **not trusted** (their span agrees with the sequence on only 61.7% of records, 5.9% for `RF00230_master.fa`; all mismatches `span > seqlen` by 1–45 nt): a ±1024-nt region is fetched and the stored sequence is located inside it by **exact string match**, anchored iff it occurs **exactly once** (fail closed — 0/>1 hits recorded with a reason). A rate-limited (3 req/s anon; 10 req/s with the run's `NCBI_API_KEY`) resumable concurrent fetch; a permanent HTTP-4xx rejection is a distinct non-retried `unavailable` status.

**Outputs.** `data/interim/flank_context/context_v0.parquet` (DVC; per-record `strand, region_start/stop, locus_offset, locus_length, lead/trail_flank, clipped_start/end, status, pad_nt, context_seq`) + git-tracked `context_v0.provenance.json`; `data/external/ncbi_flank/provenance.json` (§10.2 source manifest); measured `reports/p2/flank_context.json` (digest `a25c02df…`). Fetch cache (60 MB) gitignored as a resumable intermediate.

**Validation.** anchor rate **0.99987** (23,532 / 23,535 anchored) — 2 `multi_hit` (locus repeats within its ±1024-nt window: `NGLG01000001.1`, `NGLG01000002.1`) + 1 `unavailable` (`CP016400.1`, a suppressed NCBI accession — esearch still indexes it but the sequence is withdrawn, so every fetch 400s). Well above the code-frozen `MIN_ANCHOR_RATE` 0.95 and `MAX_FETCH_FAILED_RATE` 0.01 floors (a breach writes nothing and routes to §7). 23,253 records are full-1024-window-capable; 841/704 clipped at a contig start/end (PRD §6 zero-flank-and-flag). 99 unit tests green; full bare suite 864 passed / 133 skipped, no regression; ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 + `snakemake --lint` + `-n` clean.

**Decisions.** No ADR — implements PRD §6 as pinned (no deviation). No env amendment — `biopython=1.87` already in `envs/data.yml` (ADR-0002 D8 untriggered). The committed split table is untouched, so the §8.2 full-corpus no-leakage test is unaffected. Hardened by a pre-commit adversarial 6-lens review (53 agents; 47 raw → 25 confirmed / 22 refuted): `pad_nt` re-derived from the rows (was the one asserted clause — the P1-15/P1-16 lesson again), `validate_report` made total, fetch errors surfaced, retries metered by the limiter, a socket timeout added, the external provenance declared a rule output. **Commit.** authoring `bb03ceb` (branch `p2-00-flank-context`) + this measured-result commit; reconciled to the squash SHA at the P2 exit gate.

## Step P2-01 — 2026-07-16: build the Stage-1 window datamodule + curriculum/sampling

**Inputs.** `data/interim/flank_context/context_v0.parquet` (P2-00 — `context_seq`, `locus_offset`, `locus_length`, `clipped_start/end`, `status`), `data/processed/labels/labels_v0.parquet` (P0-20 — `label_string`, `cognate_aa`), and the git-LFS `data/processed/splits/split_assignments.parquet` (P0-23 — `klass`, `resolved_phylum`, `cluster_id`, `nested_train` + the six `FOLD_SCHEME_COLUMNS`). All three key on the same 64-hex corpus record hash; the join was verified exact (23,535 ↔ 23,535 ↔ 23,535 corpus rows, `record_id == corpus_record_sha256`) and `len(label_string) == locus_length` holds on **100%** of records, so labels are co-extensive with the locus inside `context_seq` as imp.md states.

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/data/window_dataset.py::Stage1WindowDataset` (no Snakemake rule — P2-01 ships a library + a config, not a derived artifact; the rule arrives with the P2-04 entrypoint). A window is placed by its *lead* — the count of window positions preceding the locus — so offset augmentation is a draw over `lead`, and the locus sits at `[lead, lead+locus_length)`. Labels follow imp.md exactly: the locus contributes `label_string`, **every real-DNA flank position is `background`**, and only zero-flanked positions take `IGNORE_INDEX`. The load-bearing addition is `window_lead_range`, which bounds the honest phase range: a window may step past an end of `context_seq` **only** where P2-00 flagged a real contig boundary (`clipped_start`/`clipped_end`) — otherwise the missing sequence is an artefact of our ±1024-nt fetch, and padding it would fabricate a contig end (PRD §5/§10.3, the same reasoning that made P2-00 source real flank rather than synthesise it). `Stage1WindowDataset` is torch-free and duck-typed for `DataLoader` (a map-style dataset needs only `__len__`/`__getitem__`), keeping the whole curriculum unit-testable in bare CI; `collate_windows` (lazy torch) batches to tensors. Curriculum = three multiplied inverse-frequency terms (phylum / klass / specifier AA), each `(1/n_stratum)**alpha`, drawn by a seeded torch-free `WeightedIndexSampler`.

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/data/window_dataset.py` (`Stage1WindowDataset` entry; `window_lead_range`/`carve_window`/`tile_windows`; `reverse_complement_ids`; `inverse_frequency_weights`/`curriculum_weights`; `WeightedIndexSampler`; `load_corpus_records`; `windows_digest`; `provenance_rows`; `collate_windows`) · `conf/data/stage1.yaml` (Hydra **group option** — no package directive, no defaults list; not wired until P2-04) · `tests/unit/test_window_dataset.py` (87) · `tests/golden/test_window_labels.py` (8) + `tests/fixtures/window_labels/{context_sample.csv (56 real records), expected.sha256 = 38cf7819…}`. No DVC artifact and no new data (a library step).

**Validation.** **PASS.** Golden digest `38cf7819…` reproduced by **two independent paths** (the module carve, and an inline stdlib re-derivation sharing no module code) — the `test_archiveii_lofo` precedent; an anti-tautology test confirms a 1-nt label shift moves it. Full-corpus **`TBOX_REQUIRE_NO_LEAKAGE=1` → 25/25 PASS** (CLAUDE.md §8.5 mandatory re-run; the committed split table is untouched — P2-01 augments in-memory and mints no new record identity, so the partition is unchanged). Suite **1235 passed / 86 skipped** vs a **1144 / 82** baseline re-measured on `main` = **+91 new, zero regression** (the worktree's 5 extra "artifact not materialized" skips were traced to un-copied DVC files, not code, and vanish once materialised). Torch tier: **87/87 with 0 skips** under the pinned torch 2.7.1+cu128 (`tbox-ml-dna`), so the `DataLoader` duck-typing is verified against real torch, not asserted. ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 + `snakemake --lint`/`-n` clean.

**Decisions.** No ADR — implements PRD §6/§8/§11 + ADR-0004 D5/D7 as pinned. **(1) The 3 non-`ok` P2-00 records are dropped, by reason, not native-padded** (imp.md delegated the choice): they are fetch/anchor failures, not real contig ends, so zero-flanking them would assert a boundary that does not exist (§10.3) — and they are unanchored anyway (`locus_offset == -1`). All 3 are already `nested_train=False`, so the training fold loses nothing; `load_corpus_records` reports `excluded_by_reason = {unanchored:multi_hit: 2, unanchored:unavailable: 1}`. **(2) `no_honest_window` never fires on the training fold** — all 8,303 records admit a window (179 eval-mode windows are legitimately zero-flanked at real contig ends). **(3) Sampling alphas are implementer choices** (`WEIGHTS_PINNED=false`; PRD §11 pins that the samplers exist, not their strength) and were **chosen on a measured sweep, not assumed** — the first draft's α=0.5 was **wrong**: the three terms *compound* across correlated axes (α=0.5 on 3 axes ≈ α=1.5 on the joint stratum), sending **22.8% of the stream to class II and 22.5% of all draws to 10 loci out of 8,303** — memorisation of the 22 unique class-II loci, not learning. α=0.25 gives 9.0× class-II enrichment (0.27%→2.39%), softens Firmicutes 98.5%→92.1%, and holds top-10 mass at 1.9%. `test_curriculum_bounds_the_mass_on_any_single_locus` pins the **consequence** (top-10 mass < 5%, max single locus < 1%) rather than the α, so any future parameterisation that re-creates the pathology fails CI. This is also why the sampler is not the class-II anti-mimicry mechanism — that rests on the class-II-CM-naive checkpoint + the P2-08 construction-powered recovery set (ADR-0005 D9). **(4) §9.1 decoys are deliberately NOT mixed in** (disclosed non-delivery, the P1-15 QLoRA precedent): imp.md's `Outputs:` scope P2-01 to the positive-window dataset + the three samplers; the ~10:1 seed ratio and hard-negative mining are P2-07/P2-09, and the P0 `gc_background` pool is a 0th-order composition null with no genomic context, so splicing it into a 1024-nt window is the very cue the honesty rule forbids. **(5) variant→parent→fold is now non-vacuous where it can be** — the CI predicate is structurally blind to a runtime augmentation (it reads only rows present in the committed table, all of which are self-parented), so `provenance_rows()` emits one variant→parent row per index and the unit tier runs the same predicate over them, plus a bite-test on a corrupted fold. Augmentation reaches only `nested_train` parents because `load_corpus_records(training_fold_only=True)` never loads a held-out one (PRD §11 "constrained to training-fold parents"). **Review.** CodeRabbit CLI r1 was a **real review** (all 7 files in `reviewedFiles`) and returned **7 findings — 4 major / 3 minor, all 7 valid and applied**; two were **genuine bugs my own tests missed**, both reproduced before fixing rather than taken on faith. **(a) `Window.lead` was wrong under `rc=True`:** reversing the array moves the locus to `window - lead - locus_length`, but the forward `lead` was reported, so `labels[lead : lead+locus_length]` read the wrong span on *every reverse-strand sample* — half the both-strand data would have trained mis-sliced. Measured: reported 137, actual 587. **(b) the more serious one — oversampling produced identical copies:** `__getitem__(index)` was a pure function of the index, so under with-replacement sampling the 9x-oversampled class-II records arrived as **9 byte-identical windows** (measured: 8 draws → 1 distinct window), defeating exactly the oversampling×offset-augmentation pairing PRD §11 exists for and driving at the memorisation α=0.25 was chosen to avoid. Fixed by making the sampler yield `(index, occurrence)` keys — `DataLoader` passes sampler keys through to `dataset[key]` verbatim — and folding `occurrence` into the RNG key, so each draw is independently re-phased **and** still exactly reproducible (8 draws → 8 distinct windows, verified through a real `DataLoader` under the pinned torch). The other five: the golden tier claimed to lock the RC transform but only ever carved forward windows (added an RC case with an independently re-derived expectation); `test_sample_csv_is_byte_stable` asserted only `len(digest)==64` — a tautology (now pins the real checksum `8663f3b8…`); a `pytest.skip` on a condition that provably could never fire (now an assert); a DataLoader test that could not have caught (b); and a stale `alpha=0.5` docstring. Post-fix: **1241 passed / 87 skipped**, no-leakage **25/25**, torch tier **92/92 with 0 skips**, lint clean.

**CodeRabbit r2** (real review, all 7 files) returned **1 major, valid and applied**: the r1 RC golden case built its "independent" expectation by calling `wd.reverse_complement_ids` — the very function under test, so it would have passed whatever that function did (the same tautology class as the P1-15 `lora_config_exact` gate and the P2-00 `workers_for` test). The expectation is now derived from the fixture's raw characters via a local complement table and a per-position loop, touching no RC production helper; a sabotage check confirms a reverse-without-complement mutation **fails** it. **Commit.** authoring commit on branch `p2-01-window-datamodule` + CodeRabbit r1/r2 fix commits; reconciled to the squash SHA at the P2 exit gate.

## Step P2-02 — 2026-07-16: implement the per-nucleotide objective (focal / inverse-frequency-weighted CE + optional CRF)

**Inputs.** The per-nt 8-class targets from the P2-01 datamodule (`Stage1WindowDataset`, 1024-nt windows over the P2-00 real-flank context; `labels` carry `IGNORE_INDEX` = -100 at zero-flanked positions only), the ADR-0004 D1 class vocabulary (`tbox_finder.labels::CLASS_ORDER` / `CORE_ELEMENTS`), and the class-frequency statistics measured off that stream (below). PRD §11 for the objective; ADR-0004 D1/D6 for the taxonomy and the GATE-4 core elements.

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/train/objective.py::Stage1Loss` (no Snakemake rule — a library step, like P2-01; the rule arrives with the P2-04 entrypoint). Three composable pieces: `focal_cross_entropy` (the Lin et al. modulator `(1-p_t)^γ` with optional per-class weights and `mean`/`sum`/`none` reductions), `class_weights_from_counts` + `loss_mass_share`/`core_mass_share` (a pure, stdlib-only tier that turns measured class counts into weights and reports the *consequence*), and `left_align_for_crf` (the wiring that lets the P1-03 `LinearChainCRF` consume a P2-01 window at all). Torch is imported lazily inside functions so `tbox_finder.train` stays bare-CI importable; `Stage1Loss` is parameter-free and takes the caller's CRF module, since `SegmentationHead(use_crf=True)` owns the transition parameters. **The class frequencies were measured on the real windowed stream, not read from `labels_report.json`** — that audit report counts only nucleotides inside each locus extent, so it puts background at 31.5%, whereas the stream the loss actually sees (locus + real flank out to 1024 nt) is **78.68% background**; weights derived from the report would have been wrong for every class. Measured over all 8,303 `nested_train` windows: background 6,646,421 nt (78.68%), Stem_I 769,403 (9.11%), Stem_II 316,028 (3.74%), Terminator 235,346 (2.79%), Antiterminator 231,557 (2.74%), Stem_III 191,251 (2.26%), Discriminator 32,589 (0.39%), Specifier 24,412 (0.29%) — **background:Specifier = 272:1**, the dense-background regime PRD §11's objective exists for, and Specifier is both the rarest class and a GATE-4-graded core element.

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/train/objective.py` (`Stage1Loss` entry + `Stage1LossConfig`; `focal_cross_entropy`; `class_weights_from_counts`; `loss_mass_share`/`core_mass_share`; `left_align_for_crf`; `diagnostics()`), `tests/unit/test_objective.py` (79: 34 bare + 45 torch), `src/tbox_finder/train/stage1_smoke.py` (the P1-06 `focal_cross_entropy` now **delegates** to the promoted implementation — one focal, not two), `src/tbox_finder/train/__init__.py` (`__all__`). No new data artifact, so **no DVC**; no report JSON — the class counts depend on the datamodule's fold/config, so recomputing them from the configured stream at P2-04 is more correct than reading a file that would rot, and the measured values are pinned as literals in the test as a drift guard.

**Validation.** **PASS — unit green** (the imp.md gate). **79 passed** in the objective tier; full suite **1,275 passed / 132 skipped** bare (CI-exact stack) vs a **1,241 / 87** baseline re-measured on `main` = **+34 bare +45 torch, zero regression**; **torch tier 1,390 passed / 17 skipped** under the pinned torch 2.7.1+cu128 (`tbox-ml-dna`), so every tensor assertion is verified rather than skipped. The imp.md gate "focal γ=0 ≡ weighted CE within `atol`" is graded against **`torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy` itself** — an external reference — and an anti-tautology test confirms the assertion **bites** when the denominator convention is wrong; γ>0 is pinned by an **independent closed-form derivation in stdlib `math`** (weighted and unweighted), which is the check that comparing against a sibling implementation could never provide. `TBOX_REQUIRE_NO_LEAKAGE=1` → **25/25** (§8.5 re-run; the committed split table is untouched — a pure library step mints no record identity). ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 + `snakemake --lint`/`-n` clean.

**Decisions.** **No ADR — and the absence is the point.** imp.md points at ADR-0005 for "objective/curriculum pins", but ADR-0005 pins **nothing** about the Stage-1 objective: D16 covers the *Stage-2* aux-loss weighting and D18's delegation map never assigns a Stage-1 γ, class-weight exponent, or CRF weight. So every value in `Stage1LossConfig` is an **implementer default on a swept axis** (PRD §11 → P2-06 for γ/α, P2-12 for the CRF head ablation), recorded as `diagnostics()["pinned"] = False` on the P1-15/P1-16 precedent rather than dressed up as a decision. **(1) The default expresses "focal OR inverse-frequency", not both** (γ=2, α=0): PRD §11 offers them as alternatives, Lin et al. report the two interact (α is best reduced as γ rises), and the P2-01 sampler is already a third corrective on the same imbalance — stacking all three at full strength is the compounding pathology P2-01 measured. **(2) The guard is the consequence, not the exponent** (the P2-01 sampler lesson): `loss_mass_share` reports what a weighting *does*, and it surfaces a fact worth knowing before P2-06 — at α=1 every class takes exactly 1/8 of the loss mass **by construction**, which hands **half the loss to the four classes ADR-0004 D6 excludes from GATE-4** as label-noise-prone (Stem_II, Stem_III, Terminator, Discriminator) while the three graded core elements share 3/8. Reported, never gated: no ADR pins a floor, and inventing one would be the §7 violation. Its scope is stated — a static count×weight prior, γ-independent by construction, not a gradient attribution. **(3) Zero counts raise** rather than emitting `inf` (§10.3: report the gap, don't clamp it away).

**The CRF precondition was a real defect, not a hypothetical.** TODO.md flagged that `LinearChainCRF` requires a left-aligned mask and that a zero-flanked-at-the-start window would violate it. It does: `_validate` raises unless `mask[:, 0]` is all-True, and **87 of 8,303 training windows (1.05%) are zero-flanked at a contig start**, so wiring `real_mask` straight in would raise on ~1 window in 95 — and the CRF has no `ignore_index`, so a raw `-100` tag would also index out of bounds. `left_align_for_crf` rolls each sequence so its real run starts at t=0. The roll is **exact rather than a fudge**, resting on a measured fact: zero-flanking only ever occurs at a contig end, so the real run is a single contiguous block (**0 of 8,303** windows had an interior gap), and a circular roll preserves order within it while the wrapped pad lands under the mask. The contiguity precondition is re-checked per batch and raises rather than trusting the measurement to hold forever, and a test pins that the same chain scores identically wherever it was carved. The CRF term is normalised to a **per-token** NLL — the CRF's own reduction is per-*sequence*, whose magnitude scales with L (~1024), so an un-normalised term would swamp the per-token CE by ~3 orders of magnitude and make `crf_weight` uninterpretable.

**⚠️ DURABLE (the step's real yield — a silent NaN that a measurement, not a test, caught).** A pre-commit read-only adversarial 6-lens review (63 agents; **19 raw → 2 confirmed / 17 refuted**, both survivors the same root cause found independently by two lenses) surfaced that **`focal_cross_entropy` silently emitted NaN gradients for 0 < γ < 1 — the exact axis P2-06 sweeps.** A confidently-correct position drives `ce` to *exactly* 0.0 (measured on this call path: a correct-class logit gap of **18** in both bf16 and fp32, 38 in fp64), so `1-p_t` is exactly 0; `pow`'s backward is `γ·x^(γ-1)`, which is `inf` at x=0 for γ<1, and `grad_out` there is `ce = 0` — so `inf · 0 = nan`, **while the forward loss stays finite**. The NaN then floods every upstream parameter. γ<1 is squarely on the swept axis (Lin et al.'s own grid includes 0.5), so a P2-06 sweep would have reported a plausible finite loss curve while writing NaN into the backbone — the silent-wrong-result class §10.3 forbids. Fixed by clamping the modulator base to `finfo(dtype).eps`: forward values are **bit-identical** and gradients **bit-identical at γ ∈ {0, 1, 2}** (both pinned by tests), so nothing about the default objective or the P1-06 γ=2 gate moves. **The defect was inherited from P1-06** (`stage1_smoke.focal_cross_entropy` carried the identical line), which is why that function now **delegates** to the promoted one — TODO.md's "reuse/promote rather than re-implement" turned out to be a correctness instruction, not a tidiness one: two copies would have meant fixing one and leaving the other. **My own tests structurally could not have caught it** — the fixture uses fp64 `randn` logits (gaps ~5, never near 38), the only γ<1 case was forward-only, and every `backward()` test pinned γ=2. Two further lessons: **a decorator-level `torch.float32` in a `parametrize` broke bare-CI collection** for the whole file (decorator args evaluate at collection time, when torch is `None`; `skipif` is applied too late to help) — caught only by running the real bare tier, not the torch one; and **`isinstance(value, int)` rejects `np.int64`**, so the first draft's count validator would have refused the output of `np.bincount` — the natural way P2-04 will tally these — while still needing to reject `bool` (an `int` subclass). `operator.index` accepts numpy integers and rejects `np.bool_`/float/str, with an explicit `bool` guard retained.

**Disclosed non-delivery.** The CRF is implemented and tested but **off by default** (`use_crf=False`) — imp.md scopes it as optional and P2-12 owns the linear-vs-CRF head ablation. Hard-negative/decoy mixing is not the objective's concern (P2-07/P2-09). No γ or α is tuned here: P2-06 owns the sweep, and tuning against anything before the P2 split ladder would be the circularity PRD §5 forbids. Feeds **P2-04** (which wires `Stage1LossConfig` into the Hydra entrypoint and supplies `class_counts` from the configured stream) and **P2-06** (the γ/α sweep, now safe to run below γ=1).

**Commit.** authoring commit on branch `p2-02-objective`; reconciled to the squash SHA at the P2 exit gate.


## Step P2-03 — 2026-07-16: implement + freeze the overlapping-window logit-reconciliation operator

**Inputs.** Per-position 8-class logits from every covering window of a sequence, plus the window start offsets from P2-01's `window_dataset::tile_windows` (tail-anchored; `[0]` with a single overhanging window when `seq_len <= window`). The canonical class vocabulary is `tbox_finder.labels::CLASS_ORDER` (8 classes). Pinned by **ADR-0005 D3** (the locus-construction rule) and **PRD §6 / §13.1**. No trained Stage-1 checkpoint exists yet (P2-04), so the operator is exercised on synthetic logits over real tiling geometry.

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/infer/reconcile.py::reconcile_windows` — a new `infer/` subpackage for the post-model machinery (numpy-only, torch-free, bare-CI importable; a CPU `torch.Tensor` bridges through `numpy.asarray`). The operator is the whole step and stops where the step stops: at the per-position arg-max, *before* any along-sequence merging (the rest of the D3 rule — threshold scope, minimum span, gap-merge, co-occurrence, flank — is later). The reduction is a two-pass max-shifted accumulation: per-window `log_softmax` on the in-bounds slice, a running per-position max, then `log_probs = max + log(sum(exp(x - max)) / coverage)`. There is no numpy log-sum-exp anywhere in the repo (the only prior art is `models/seg_head.py`'s `torch.logsumexp` inside the CRF forward algorithm, not reusable here), so stable `logsumexp`/`log_softmax` are written and tested against stdlib `math` including the overflow and underflow regimes where the naive form dies.

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/infer/__init__.py` + `src/tbox_finder/infer/reconcile.py` (`reconcile_windows` entry; `Reconciled` = `log_probs`/`prediction`/`coverage`/`zero_flanked`; `logsumexp`; `log_softmax`; `diagnostics()`), `tests/unit/test_reconcile.py` (62), `tests/golden/test_reconcile_golden.py` (16) + `tests/fixtures/reconcile/{geometry.csv, expected.sha256}`, **ADR-0005 Amendment A3**. No data artifact, so **no DVC**; no report JSON (the operator is parameter-free — there is no measured number to record). Note the golden is `test_reconcile_golden.py`, not imp.md's literal `test_reconcile.py`: pytest cannot import two same-basename test modules from `tests/unit` and `tests/golden` without `__init__.py`, and the repo's existing convention already resolves this (`test_labels_golden.py`, `test_decoys_golden.py`). Running the two files separately hides the collision; only the full-suite run surfaces it.

**Validation.** **PASS — unit + golden green** (the imp.md gate). Full suite **1,365 passed / 134 skipped** (CI-exact stack, all four anti-rot vars armed) vs a **1,287 / 133** baseline re-measured on `main` with the identical command = **+78 new, zero regression**; the +1 skip is the torch-tier handoff test, which CI installs no torch for. `TBOX_REQUIRE_NO_LEAKAGE=1` → **25/25** (§8.5 re-run; the committed split table is untouched — a pure library step mints no record identity). ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 + `snakemake --lint`/`-n` clean. Under the pinned torch 2.7.1+cu128 (`tbox-ml-dna`, which carries no pytest — verified by direct execution): a CPU `torch.Tensor` reconciles **bit-identically** to the numpy path, float32 logits agree with float64 to 7.9e-07 with **1.000 argmax agreement**, and the operator matches an **independent derivation built from torch's own `log_softmax`/`logsumexp`** to **3.6e-15** — an external reference, not a comparison against itself.

**Decisions — the step's central question was a §7 stop, and it was NOT mine to close.** D3 pins that "the per-position 8-class **logits** ... are **averaged in log-sum-exp space then arg-maxed**". That sentence admits **two coherent operators**: the literal reading (`argmax_c log(mean_w exp(logits))`, a soft-max pooling over raw logits) and the per-window-normalised reading (`argmax_c log(mean_w softmax(logits))`, the mean of per-window posteriors). My first draft implemented the second and **self-adjudicated it in a docstring** as "a concretisation, not a departure". That was wrong on process and the justification was partly wrong on substance: I argued the normalised form was *required* because the D3 threshold and the §11 ECE stack need a proper distribution — but the literal form's output can also be softmaxed over `c`, giving a distribution with the same arg-max, so that argument does not discriminate between them. **They are not notational variants:** measured on the golden's own real pinned-tiling geometries, **3,418 of 8,931 multi-covered positions — 38.3% — receive a different class** under the two readings. Since that arg-max feeds locus construction, it moves GATE-4 boundary IoU and the D3 per-locus recall floor — the very quantities the operator was pinned to protect. Escalated to the user with both forms and the measured divergence; **user sign-off 2026-07-16 chose the per-window-normalised form**, recorded as **ADR-0005 Amendment A3** with the discriminating rationale: `exp(logit)` is an unnormalised score, so pooling it weights window `w` by its partition function `Z_w` — a quantity the training objective **never constrains**, because the softmax inside the cross-entropy is invariant to a constant shift of a position's logits. Two windows predicting the *identical* posterior would contribute unequally under the literal form. `diagnostics()` now cites `D3 + A3` rather than claiming D3 authority for a reading D3 does not contain.

**What was never at issue.** The **coverage normalisation** (`− log |W(p)|`) is common to both readings: it is what "averaged" means as against "summed", and D3's own stated purpose (seam-freeness, "boundary IoU is not a 512-grid artifact") *requires* it — without it an interior nucleotide (coverage 2–3) would outscore a tail nucleotide (coverage 1) on identical evidence. The arithmetic divides *inside* the log so coverage-invariance is **bit-exact**, not merely close. **Contig ends**: a window overhanging either end is zero-padded by the caller and the model still emits logits over that pad — logits that describe no DNA. Those are sliced off *before* any arithmetic (not masked afterwards), and every real position covered by such a window is flagged. Under the pinned tail-anchored tiling this fires **only** for sequences shorter than one window; `seq_len == window` fits exactly and is correctly **not** flagged. A tiling **gap raises** rather than silently calling an unscored nucleotide `background` (§10.3).

**⚠️ DURABLE (the step's real yield — my test fixture was near-vacuous and I only found it by sabotage).** A read-only adversarial 6-lens review (57 agents; **17 raw → 3 confirmed / 14 refuted**) flagged that the **left-overhang (negative-start) slice arithmetic had zero numeric coverage**: `logits[k, lo - start : hi - start]` has a non-zero left offset *only* when `start < 0`, `tile_windows` never emits one, and my two negative-start tests asserted only `zero_flanked` and `coverage` — never a value. I verified it by **sabotage rather than by reading**: an operator misaligning *every* left-overhanging window by `-start` nucleotides **passed all 48 unit tests**. Chasing why the new numeric test still did not bite exposed something worse — **the fixture generator itself was degenerate.** It keyed a cheap polynomial hash on `f"{tag}|{w}|{j}|{c}"` with the class index **last**, so the eight class logits at a position landed within **1.3e-08** of each other; `log_softmax` annihilates a per-position constant **exactly**, so every position softmaxed to a **uniform 0.125** and the entire suite was comparing uniform distributions to uniform distributions. That is the [[vacuous-test-perturbations]] lesson one level down: not a vacuous *assertion*, but a fixture that **cannot express the thing the assertions measure** — and it made bit-exactness claims pass for the wrong reason. Both fixed (SHAKE-256 with an independent draw per class), and `test_the_logit_fixture_is_expressive_not_uniform` now fails if a generator ever regresses to it; the sabotage is now caught by 3 of 4 parametrized cases (correctly not the 4th — right-overhang-only has a zero left offset). Three more, all mine: **`np.argmax` treats NaN as the maximum**, so `|logit| ~ 1e308` overflowed the log-softmax shift to `-inf`, `-inf - -inf` gave NaN, and the operator **returned class 1 where the true arg-max was class 0** — a silently wrong call, now fail-closed; the public `logsumexp` returned NaN instead of the correct limit for all-`-inf` / `+inf` rows; and **`np.asarray([True, 5])` promotes to int64**, so the `bool` guard on `starts` was defeated by a mixed list while the pure-`[True]` case coincidentally survived promotion and kept its test green.

**⚠️ Claim accuracy (§10.2) — I stated sample properties as corpus facts.** `geometry.csv` called 366 the "shortest real context", 2556 the "longest", and 2412 "a modal real length (2 records)". Checked against `context_v0.parquet` (n = 23,535): the real minimum non-zero context is **183**, the maximum **2598**, and 2412 occurs **34×** while the modal length is **2327**, with **283** records — all three were properties of the 56-row `context_sample.csv`, not of the corpus, and no test read the `note` column. Corrected to sample-scoped wording (`sampled_real_context_length`), the true corpus range recorded, and the golden's honesty scope now states that the membership check is against the committed 56-row sample — which is what a bare CI tier can read, since `context_v0.parquet` is DVC-tracked and absent there.

**The golden's honesty scope, stated.** The geometries are **real sampled** context lengths; the **logits are synthetic**, and cannot be otherwise — the operator is pure arithmetic over logits, content-independent, and no Stage-1 checkpoint exists before P2-04. This golden pins the operator's *arithmetic* and asserts **nothing biological** (the P1-14 synthetic-RNA-latency-probe precedent, §10.3). The stream is **SHAKE-256** (FIPS 202), deliberately **not** `numpy.random.default_rng`: NumPy does not guarantee `Generator` stream stability across releases, so an RNG-seeded fixture could drift under a numpy bump and then be silently "repaired" by regenerating the digest. The digest quantises `log_probs` to 1e-6 (fp noise is ~1e-15; any semantic change moves them by orders more) and forces big-endian dtypes so it cannot depend on host byte order. `geometry.csv` is byte-pinned by a hard-coded sha256 so an input edit cannot be laundered. The committed digest `08f088c9…` was **produced by two independent paths agreeing** — the module, and a stdlib `math` re-derivation with explicit per-position loops that shares nothing with it — not by blessing whatever the module emitted. Every load-bearing property carries an **anti-tautology partner** that proves it bites: coverage-invariance against a bare log-sum-exp (which drifts by exactly `log 2` at coverage 2), per-window shift-invariance against a raw-logit reduction (non-vacuous only because the shift hits *one of two* windows before averaging — softmax would otherwise absorb it), and the golden's two sabotage runs (`no_coverage_norm`, `raw_logits`) which must both miss the digest while a `faithful` run through the same harness reproduces it.

**Disclosed non-delivery.** Only the reconciliation operator lands: along-sequence locus construction (per-class-vs-global threshold scope, minimum span, gap-merge distance, recall-favouring co-occurrence, flank size) is the rest of the D3 rule and is not P2-03's scope; the Stage-1 threshold *value* is frozen at the phase gate on real posteriors, not here. Feeds **P2-14** (GATE-4 boundary IoU under overlapping tiles) and **P5** (the scan).

**Commit.** authoring commit on branch `p2-03-reconcile`; reconciled to the squash SHA at the P2 exit gate.

## Step P2-04 — 2026-07-16: assemble the Stage-1 training entrypoint (Hydra + DDP + W&B-offline)

**Inputs.** The three P2 halves that already existed separately: P2-01's `data/window_dataset.py` (1024-nt windows over the real P2-00 flank context, curriculum sampling, offset + strand augmentation) with its deliberately-unwired `conf/data/stage1.yaml`; P2-02's `train/objective.py::Stage1Loss` (focal CE + optional inverse-frequency weighting + optional CRF); and P1-04's `models/stage1_segmenter.py::Stage1Segmenter` (Caduceus-PS `d89eeb85` → non-averaged RC-combine → 8-class per-position head). Pinned by **PRD §10.1/§10.3/§11/§16**, **ADR-0002** (D7 full-FT default, A2 C2 kernel, A4 env, A6/A7 determinism) and **ADR-0005** (D3 window/stride + 8-class, D15 non-averaged RC-combine).

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/train/train_stage1.py` wires; it re-implements nothing. It owns the four things the three halves cannot own individually: the Hydra config surface (`conf/train/stage1.yaml`, a primary carrying the package-global directive plus leading-slash defaults so it reaches `/data`, `/model`, `/tracking`, `/optim`), the gradient-checkpointing wiring, the DDP shard (`ShardedSampler`), and the run's §11 provenance record. `conf/optim/stage1.yaml` is new and deliberately distinct from `adamw_smoke.yaml`, which belongs to P1-07's 300-locus fixture and should not lend its learning rate to a real run. **`set_determinism` was promoted** into `train/repro.py` — the module that already owns the ADR-0002 A7 determinism contract — and `stage1_smoke.set_determinism` now delegates: two recipes that drifted apart would leave the A7 gate green while it silently measured two different protocols (the P2-02 `focal_cross_entropy` precedent). Class counts are computed **from the configured stream**, never read from `labels_report.json`, whose locus-only totals put background at 31.5%.

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/train/train_stage1.py` (`main` Hydra entry → `train_stage1`; `enable_gradient_checkpointing`; `ShardedSampler`; `compute_class_counts`; `build_report`/`derive_clauses`/`validate_report`) · `conf/train/stage1.yaml` + `conf/optim/stage1.yaml` · `tests/ml/test_train_smoke.py` (45 tests, 3 tiers) · measured `reports/p2/train_stage1_smoke.json` (git) · `train/repro.py` + `train/stage1_smoke.py` (the promotion) · `tests/unit/test_window_dataset.py` (the P2-01 wiring guard, inverted not deleted) · `.gitignore` (Hydra job logs). **No DVC** — the report is git-tracked and no checkpoint is saved (`save_checkpoint: false`; P2-09 owns the production checkpoint and its DVC pointer).

**Validation.** **PASS — the imp.md gate is "smoke green; `tests/ml/test_eval_gate.py` green"**, both met. Full suite **1,406 passed / 143 skipped** (CI-exact stack, every gate armed incl. the new `TBOX_REQUIRE_TRAIN_SMOKE`) against a **1,366 / 134** baseline re-measured on this branch = **+40 new, zero regression**; the +7 skips are the torch/Hydra/CUDA tiers, which CI installs neither torch nor hydra for. Under the **pinned** stack (torch 2.7.1+cu128, `tbox-ml-dna`) all three tiers run and the count is **49 passed / 0 skipped**, the end-to-end CUDA train step included. A useful discovery for later steps: `uv run --python <the conda env's python> --with pytest==9.0.2 --no-project` layers pytest over the **pinned** interpreter, so the torch tier is exercised under the real ADR-0002 stack rather than an ad-hoc torch — `tbox-ml-dna` itself carries no pytest, and P2-03 had to fall back to direct execution for this reason. The end-to-end CUDA train step composes on the real stream. `TBOX_REQUIRE_NO_LEAKAGE=1` → **25/25** (the committed split table is untouched — a wiring step mints no record identity). ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 + `snakemake --lint`/`-n` clean.

**Decisions — the step's central question was a §7 stop, and it was not mine to close.** PRD §10.3 pins *"full fine-tune fits one GPU with gradient checkpointing; DDP×8 for throughput"*, and imp.md lists gradient checkpointing as an entrypoint deliverable. **The pinned backbone cannot honour it through the standard path**, measured against revision `d89eeb85`: `supports_gradient_checkpointing` is `False`, `gradient_checkpointing_enable()` raises `ValueError: Caduceus does not support gradient checkpointing`, the string appears nowhere in the remote-code class, and `modeling_caduceus.py` carries a literal `# TODO: Add support for gradient checkpointing` (line 228, inside the layer loop, directly above the layer call). `_set_gradient_checkpointing` — the hook HF's machinery needs — is absent from the module entirely, so there is nothing to hook. Since the PRD sits at the top of the precedence stack this was escalated rather than absorbed, with the measurement attached: hand-wiring it is both possible and cheap, and the footprint is not obviously the binding constraint (batch 16 peaked at **8.554 GiB** — but see the device caveat below: that is a **laptop RTX 4060** number, and the A4000 is P2-05's to measure). **User sign-off 2026-07-16 chose to hand-wire it, default ON**, which honours the pin literally and needs no deviation. Each of the 16 `RCPSMambaBlock`s is wrapped in `torch.utils.checkpoint(use_reentrant=False)` — the **ADR-0002 A2 C2 idiom** verbatim, *"a code change, not a flag"*, which that amendment already applies to the absent selective-scan fallback.

**Measured (mechanics, not science) — and read the device line before the numbers.** Full fine-tune, 1024 nt, AdamW step, **laptop RTX 4060 (8 GiB, sm_89)**, `tbox-ml-dna`: peak VRAM **0.813 / 1.328 / 2.358 / 4.424 / 8.554 GiB** at batch **1 / 2 / 4 / 8 / 16** without checkpointing; **0.961 GiB** at batch 8 **with** it — a **4.6×** cut — gradients finite throughout and the loss moving **1.5939 → 1.5940**, i.e. the same computation rather than a broken wrap. The committed smoke reports **0.482 GiB** at batch 2 with checkpointing, on the same card (the report now records `hardware.device_name`, so the comparison is attributable rather than assumed).

**Three caveats on that sweep, none of them cosmetic.** (i) **It is a 4060, not an A4000.** An earlier draft of this stanza wrote *"batch 16 peaks at 8.554 GiB on a 16 GiB A4000"* — a number measured on the dev box, asserted as a property of the target device, which is the **P2-03 §10.2 lesson repeating one step later** (sample properties worded as corpus facts). No A4000 measurement exists for P2-04; **P2-05 owns it**, and the extrapolation is a prediction, not a result. (ii) **The 8.554 GiB figure exceeds the 8 GiB card it was measured on** — `max_memory_allocated` is a real allocator count, but on WSL2 the driver spills into host memory rather than OOMing, so that point is not a native-VRAM measurement and should not be read as one. (iii) **The sweep lives in no artifact.** Every number above comes from a scratchpad probe; only the batch-2 point is reproducible from `reports/p2/train_stage1_smoke.json`. They informed a §7 decision and are recorded here for that reason, but a reader cannot re-derive them from the repo — P2-05's sizing report is where these belong, on the right card. **The throughput cost is deliberately unmeasured and unclaimed**: each batch ran in its own process (a CUDA OOM poisons the context, and on this WSL2 box an OOM surfaces as a bare `RuntimeError`, not `torch.cuda.OutOfMemoryError`), so single-step timings are warmup-dominated noise. **P2-05 owns the A4000 footprint** and may propose flipping the default with an ADR note. One interaction is disclosed rather than buried: checkpointing recomputes the forward during the backward, and the Mamba `selective_scan_cuda` kernel registers no deterministic algorithm (A2 C2), so gradients are taken w.r.t. activations that may differ from the forward's in the last bits. A7's contract is already metric-level rather than bitwise, so this sits inside the pinned tolerance — but it is real, and P2-05/P2-06 should watch it.

**Two imp.md deliverables already existed under other names, and the honest move was to reuse.** `conf/model/caduceus_ps.yaml` → `conf/model/caduceus_stage1.yaml` already carries `repo_id` + the pinned revision; a second file would give a load-bearing pin two homes to drift between. `workflow/rules/train.smk::wandb_sync` → `setup.smk::wandb_sync` has existed since P0; a duplicate rule name breaks the Snakefile's `rules/*.smk` auto-include, and the Snakefile states outright that GPU stages are **intentionally not in the DAG** (PRD §16 — the SLURM executor would bypass the `--test-only` preflight and the no-auto-resubmit rule). So there is no `train.smk` and should not be. Both facts are now **locked by tests** (`test_wandb_sync_rule_is_not_duplicated`, `test_the_backbone_pin_has_exactly_one_home`) rather than left in prose where the next reader re-litigates them. The P2-01 guard asserting `conf/data/stage1.yaml` was *unwired* was **inverted, not deleted** — P2-04 is the step it was waiting for, and deleting it would leave the wiring unguarded.

**DURABLE — a no-op stub passed every test in bare CI, and only sabotage showed it.** Following the P2-03 discipline I sabotaged each guard rather than reading it. Dropping `- /data: stage1`, dropping the package directive, and flipping the checkpointing default all failed correctly. But replacing `enable_gradient_checkpointing` with `return 0` — **the exact §10.3 stub the function exists to avoid** — **passed 3/3 checkpoint tests in the bare environment**, because the two tests that catch it live in the torch tier, and CI installs no torch. The tier that guards the claim is the tier that cannot run where the claim is checked. Two responses: the wrap is now proven by **counting forward invocations** (`calls == 2` under checkpointing vs `1` without — recomputation is what makes the wrap real, so counting it is the only assertion a no-op cannot satisfy), and the committed report records `n_blocks_wrapped == n_blocks == 16` as a **re-derived** clause. **Stated plainly: bare CI still cannot execute the wrap.** Only the local/cluster torch tier can, and it must be run as the §8.5 manual gate — with the sabotage now confirmed to fail it (2 failed). A second find, also mine: `provenance_complete` read `a and b and c or d`, which binds as `(a and b and c) or d` and would have certified provenance on `seed == 0` alone whatever the git SHA said; the test pinning it now asserts seed 0 is legal *and* that a missing SHA still fails.

**DURABLE — CodeRabbit r1 found a deadlock my test had written into the contract.** Seven findings (1 critical, 5 major, 1 minor); **six applied, one reasoned skip**, all ten files in `reviewedFiles`. Three were real bugs, and the pattern joining them is that each *looked* handled. **(1) The critical one: `ShardedSampler` deadlocked DDP.** A bare `rank::world_size` stride shards 23 draws over 4 ranks as **6/6/6/5**; the short rank runs one fewer backward, leaves the gradient all-reduce, and the other three block on a collective that can never complete — the job **hangs** rather than failing, which is the worst available outcome. It is now truncated to a multiple of `world_size` *before* striding. The part worth keeping: **my own test asserted the union over ranks equalled the full stream**, and that equality *requires* the ragged shards — the test did not miss the bug, it **specified** it. Being green was evidence of nothing, because the invariant itself was wrong. **(2) `PYTHONHASHSEED` was set in-process, which is a no-op.** CPython fixes hash randomisation while initialising the interpreter, so `os.environ["PYTHONHASHSEED"] = "0"` inside the run mutated a variable nothing would read again — PRD §11's determinism requirement discharged in appearance only. That is the **same shape as the gradient-checkpointing stub this very step exists to avoid**, which I wrote three hundred lines above it and still did not recognise. Replaced with `check_pythonhashseed`, which *verifies* the launcher's value and raises. **(3) The `/data` group never reached the datamodule.** `build_stream` constructed `Stage1DataConfig(seed=…)` alone, silently dropping the composed group — the defaults merely happened to match, so a PRD §11 window/stride sweep (P2-06) would have run every point on the same 1024/512 stream while faithfully reporting the swept value. **My Hydra test was vacuous on precisely this point**: it asserted `cfg["data"]["window_nt"] == 1024` on the *composed dict*, which proves only that Hydra read the file and says nothing about what the dataset was built from. The wiring composed; it just did not connect. The test now follows an override all the way to the dataclass. Two more applied: `derive_clauses` now validates `class_counts_scope` (the §10.2 honesty field was itself unguarded — `full_stream` was asserted, not derived), and the wiring guard was anchored to the repo root rather than a CWD-relative glob that would pass vacuously off-root. **One reasoned skip:** per-job Hydra multirun output dirs — the fixed `report_path` collision is real, but P2-04 runs no multirun and P2-06 owns the sweep and its output layout; recorded in `conf/train/stage1.yaml` with a concrete override rather than pre-empted here.

**CodeRabbit r2 — three more of mine, and one is the same mistake twice.** Five findings (3 major, 2 minor), **all valid, all applied**, all ten files reviewed again. **The CUDA test satisfied its own guard.** It monkeypatched `PYTHONHASHSEED=0` so `check_pythonhashseed` would pass — which asserts a lie, because the variable then reads `"0"` while the interpreter's actual hash seed stays whatever it launched with. That is **the in-process no-op the guard exists to catch, reproduced inside the test written for it** — I fixed the bug in the source and then re-committed it, one file away, in the test. It now *verifies*, so the tier must be launched as `PYTHONHASHSEED=0 pytest …`; sabotage confirms it fails when it is not. **`provenance_complete` certified on an empty SHA**, since `isinstance("", str)` is `True` — a missing SHA wearing the right type — and the seed was recorded in two places (`provenance.seed`, `diagnostics.config.seed`) that were never cross-checked, so they could disagree while the report still certified (the P1-15/P1-16 duplicated-`peak_vram_gib` lesson, third outing). **`set_determinism` used `setdefault` for `CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG`**, so a launcher-exported bad value — the workspace-disabling `:0:0`, say — was *preserved*, leaving cuBLAS nondeterministic; and because `warn_only=True` downgrades torch's objection to a warning, the run would carry on quietly non-reproducible. It now validates against **torch's own published literals** (`:4096:8` / `:16:8`), read out of the installed torch 2.7.1 `use_deterministic_algorithms` docstring rather than asserted from memory — and worth recording, `context7`'s first hit was torch's **cuDNN-RNN** determinism note, whose `:4096:2` is a *different subsystem's* value and is **not** accepted by the cuBLAS check (Caduceus is Mamba/SSM; there is no RNN here). Checking the pinned library's own source settled it. Both minors were guards that could not fire: the `wandb_sync` check counted filenames (one `.smk` declaring the rule twice would pass), and the wiring check substring-matched raw text — so **commenting the default out**, which breaks the wiring outright, would leave the string present and the guard green precisely when it mattered.

**Self-review (§5.1 built-in rung) — a failed run exited 0.** CodeRabbit r3 hit the Fair-Usage limit, escalating **8 → 24 minutes**, so the r2 delta was self-reviewed per §5.1's built-in rung; the gate itself is satisfied by two real substantive rounds (all ten files in `reviewedFiles` both times). That pass found one more of the same family. `train_stage1` raised only on an **invalid** report — but `validate_report` checks that the clauses *follow from the evidence*, not that they *passed*. So a run training **zero steps** produced a perfectly consistent report saying `overall_pass: false` and returned normally, **exit 0**: the sbatch would look successful and §9.3's artifact-based verification (a DONE marker plus a zero-byte `.err`) would pass it, because the failure lived only in a JSON field nobody re-reads. `batch_size` exceeding the per-rank draw count reaches it in one step — which is reachable from **P2-05's sizing sweep**, the very next step, whose job is to push batch size. The report is now written first (it is the evidence) and a failed gate then raises; a checkpoint is no longer saved from a failed run. Verified by execution rather than by reading: `max_records=4 batch_size=64` raises *"gate FAILED (clauses: train_step_ran, loss_finite)"*. **A limit worth stating rather than glossing:** `test_committed_report_validates_and_its_clauses_re_derive` compares the stored gate against `derive_clauses` — but `build_report` *sealed* that gate with `derive_clauses`, so it catches report **tampering** (verified by sabotage: corrupting the committed gate fails it) and **cannot** catch a wrong derivation, which would seal and re-derive identically. What pins the derivation independently is the hand-built-report tier, where each clause is asserted **False** against evidence constructed to violate it. Neither tier is sufficient alone.

**Adversarial self-review — and the worst finding is a false claim of mine.** With r3 rate-limited, the full diff went through a read-only 3-lens review (correctness / test-adequacy / claim-accuracy; read-only **Explore** lenses, not Workflow agents — one of those silently mutated the code under review at P2-00, and the tree was audited clean afterwards). **The dev-log had said "batch 16 peaks at 8.554 GiB on a 16 GiB A4000". It was measured on the laptop RTX 4060.** No A4000 measurement exists for P2-04 — that is P2-05's — so a dev-box property was asserted as a property of the target device: **the P2-03 §10.2 lesson repeating one step later**, in the very stanza that recounts it. Two further caveats surfaced with it and are now stated: **8.554 GiB exceeds the 8 GiB card it was measured on** (WSL2 spills to host memory instead of OOMing, so that point is not a native-VRAM measurement and must not be read as one), and **the entire sweep lives in no artifact** — it came from a scratchpad probe, so the numbers that drove a §7 decision are not re-derivable from the repo; only the batch-2 point is, from the committed report. Four more of my own numbers were wrong or stale: the test count (30 → **45**), a leftover "31 passed" contradicting "46 passed", two of six commits uncited, and the report described as "result `42b2cca`" when `42b2cca`'s report was a superseded draft.

**Three real bugs, same family as the rest.** **`peak_vram_gib` was measured on the wrong device** — `reset_peak_memory_stats()`/`max_memory_allocated()` default to `current_device()`, but `set_device` runs only on the DDP path, so `device=cuda:1` (an exposed knob, and the obvious way to pick a free GPU) trained on `cuda:1` and reported `cuda:0`'s peak: **~0.0 GiB in the field the PRD §10.3 claim rests on**, self-consistently, so the validator passed it; `device=cpu` on a CUDA box reported `0.0` rather than `None`. Now device-scoped, and the report records `hardware.device_name` — every sibling P1 report carries it, this one did not, which is what left `0.482 GiB` unattributable in the first place. **`dist.destroy_process_group()` was unreachable on a raise**, so a rank that died left the survivors blocking in the next NCCL collective until the watchdog fired — the ragged-shard deadlock's shape again, different trigger; now a `finally`. And **`compute_class_counts` does not scan "at the deterministic lead"** as its docstring and the report field both claimed: with `offset_augmentation` on, `window_at` draws phase and strand from the seeded RNG. The counts are *reproducible*, not *phase-independent*, and they depend on the epoch — correct today only because the call happens to precede the `set_epoch` loop.

**Four guards that could not fire — each fix verified by sabotage, not by reading.** `ShardedSampler.set_epoch` had **zero coverage**: `def set_epoch(e): pass` was green, and `_FakeSampler` was *structurally incapable* of catching it because its `__iter__` ignored the epoch — the P2-03 degenerate-fixture lesson in a new costume. `set_determinism` had **zero coverage anywhere in the repo**, so reverting the r2 cuBLAS fix to `setdefault` was green. `hf_flag_supported` was a hard-coded `False` asserted to be `False` — a clause compared to the constant that produced it, which could never fire however the backbone changed; it is now probed off the model, so a future revision that *does* implement checkpointing trips the test rather than passing silently. And the checkpointing call-count only checked the total **after** backward, so a wrap that called the block twice **eagerly** — zero memory saving, 2× compute, the exact inverse of checkpointing — passed; it is now sampled *between* forward and backward, which is the only place recomputation is distinguishable. Also: `TBOX_REQUIRE_TRAIN_SMOKE` was **armed nowhere**, making its fail-closed branch dead code and letting all four committed-report tests skip green if the report vanished — the anti-rot contract written and then not connected.

**Provenance, and why this took two commits.** The first report was generated from a dirty tree, so its `git_sha` named `4bc9f08` — a commit that does not contain `train_stage1.py` at all. A SHA that does not describe the code that produced the run is worse than no SHA, so `git_dirty` is now recorded and `test_committed_report_was_produced_from_a_clean_tree` **fails on exactly that** (it did, before the split). The authoring/result split is the **P1-16 precedent**: the committed report carries the SHA of the commit that produced it with `git_dirty: false` — regenerated after **every** review round, so it describes the shipped code rather than a superseded draft, and the branch squashes to one atomic commit on `main`.

**Claim scope, stated.** `is_science: false` and `gate4_graded: false` are structural invariants the validator enforces, not comments. The loss falls **1.6607 → 1.6430** across four steps on a 64-record slice; that is **not evidence of learning** and is not offered as any — a segmentation model can descend on 64 records by predicting background. The committed `class_counts` cover **64 of the 8,303 `nested_train` records** and the report says so in `class_counts_scope` (`full_stream: false`), because P2-03 shipped 56-row sample properties worded as corpus facts and no test read the field that said otherwise; here a test asserts `full_stream` follows from the arithmetic. That slice's background share (**51,410 / 65,120 = 78.9%**) does land close to P2-02's **78.68%** over the full fold — a corroboration, not a corpus measurement. GATE-4 is **P2-14**, on the real leave-clade-out split.

**Disclosed non-delivery.** DDP is **built but exercised only at world_size 1**: `ShardedSampler` is unit-tested to partition the draw stream exactly across 4 ranks while preserving P2-01's `(index, occurrence)` tuples (dropping them would collapse a 9× oversampled class-II record to nine identical copies — the memorisation P2-01 measured against), but no multi-GPU run has happened; **P2-05** owns that on the A4000. No sweep (**P2-06**), no CRF ablation (**P2-12**), no decoys (**P2-07/P2-09**), and the class-II-CM-naive second checkpoint ADR-0005 D9 requires is **P2-11** — the entrypoint is parameterised over the label variant but does not train it. No `wandb sync` was run: the offline run dir is written on the compute node and `setup.smk::wandb_sync` uploads from the login node.

**Commit.** Six on branch `p2-04-train-stage1`: authoring `66f0598` → first report `42b2cca` → CodeRabbit r1 `e3702bf` → report regen `f3fc142` → r2 `a4d118c` → report regen `7c0ab67`, plus the self-review `9d6d745` and its regen. **The shipped report is the latest regeneration, not `42b2cca`** — each review round changed the code, so the report was re-run each time to keep its `git_sha` naming the code that produced it. Reconciled to the squash SHA at the P2 exit gate.
